mnemonics.title: HCAI CORE PRINCIPLES
mnemonics.content: HCAI PRINCIPLES: HUMAN CONTROL, TRANSPARENCY, FAIRNESS, ACCOUNTABILITY (Remember as H-T-F-A)

mnemonics.title: XAI KEY PROPERTIES
mnemonics.content: EXPLAINABLE AI PROPERTIES: TRANSPARENT, COMPREHENSIBLE, ACTIONABLE, TRUSTWORTHY (Remember as T-C-A-T)

mnemonics.title: BIAS DETECTION STEPS
mnemonics.content: BIAS CHECK PROCESS: DATA AUDIT, MODEL ANALYSIS, OUTPUT EVALUATION, STAKEHOLDER REVIEW (Remember as D-M-O-S)

mnemonics.title: HUMAN-OVERSIGHT LEVELS
mnemonics.content: OVERSIGHT LEVELS: HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP, HUMAN-ON-THE-LOOP, HUMAN-IN-COMMAND (Remember as L-O-O-P)

acronyms.usage: A design paradigm that prioritizes human needs and values in AI development
Framework where humans participate in AI decision-making and validation
AI systems that provide clear explanations for their decisions and actions
Openness about AI capabilities, limitations, and decision processes

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a feedback loop: AI makes decision → Human reviews → Human approves/modifies → Output finalized. Draw as circular diagram with human at decision point.
Picture a balance scale: on one side AI capability, on other side user trust. Keep them balanced—not too high (dangerous over-trust) or too low (rejected useful AI).
Think of three stacked filters: (1) Input data filter, (2) Model behavior filter, (3) Output fairness filter—each catching different bias types.
Draw as iterative cycle: Understand Human Needs → Design with Principles → Implement AI → Test & Evaluate → Gather Feedback → Refine (always returns to human needs).

analogies.analogy: Like a pilot (human) with autopilot (AI)—AI handles routine tasks, but pilot oversees, can take control, and makes critical decisions.
Like a doctor explaining diagnosis—not just giving prescription, but explaining reasoning, evidence, and alternatives for informed patient decisions.
Like learning to cross streets—neither ignoring cars (under-trust) nor refusing to cross at all (over-trust), but calibrated trust based on actual traffic patterns.
Like product manufacturers—are responsible for defects, provide warranties, face regulations even if defects are unintentional.
Like a chef and sous-chef—AI handles prep work efficiently, human chef makes final presentation decisions, combines skills for better results than either alone.
